channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 192 replies — 634.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 192 • Updated: 2026-03-28 13:11 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62032967
>>62032929
>The mind doesn't need to be the enemy
I never said it is. If the mind is your enemy then it'll fight against you. You'll never win the fight against your own mind. What you repress will grow stronger. My whole point is to observe it in a non-judgmental way. If you do that it'll start to collapse in on itself. Just by observing your mind, just by noticing it's movement it'll start to stop moving. If you're fighting it then you're interacting it. If you interact you feed the movement. Ok, let's try and experiment. Sit on your chair, start watching your mind and when a thought comes up start to FIGHT IT AS HARD AS YOU CAN. Go full force at it, try to silence is as best as you can. Do that for like 15min and your head will start to hurt and the thought will keep coming back louder and louder. Don't believe me? Good, try it yourself. Don't believe anything but the experience itself.
The nature of the mind is so that it can only do multiplications and additions. You can't substract and divide. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. MONKEY BREATHING FIRE. Let me guess? You're thinking about a monkey breathing fire, right? Funny how that works, right? You'd never thought about it if I didn't point it out, right?

>Themind can be a hindrance and be your most powerful ally
That's a though produced by your mind. It tries to make you believe it's your ally in order to keep on minding. It's neither your ally or your enemy. It just doesn't want to die and therefore it'll trick you into believing all sorts of things. Ask yourself this "Is my mind tricking me?" and then OBSERVE your mind and see what it does and how it tries to trick you. Just try. I'm not saying I'm right or wrong. Get the experience yourself.

62032974
>>62032929
>>62032967
>If you do that it'll start to collapse in on itself
This takes time and consistent effort. It doesn't JUST happen... And I'm not saying to believe my bullshit. You try it yourself and then you come to your own conclusions. DYOR. See what happens. Weird shit is going to start happening.

62032992
>>62032922
>imagine my fetish
no thanks

62033000
>>62032992
Fetish? We're talking about a pizza party with some side dish of freshly harvested young jerky.

62033138
wtf is this trash thread, it reads like a random schizos blog

62034039
>>62033138
Must be true if you say so

62034101
>>62033138
Or wait. Let's try this. Might be an interesting conversation. How so? What have I done or said to make you believe that?

62034381
7,5$ top in 2028
+400% from ATL

62034423
>>62032967
This exercise is very elementary.

>And not saying to believe my Bullshit
Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?

62034490
>>62034423
>Do you live in a bubble purposely living in blissful ignorance to the outside world so you can be content?
How so? What makes you think that?

62034856
>>62034423
>This exercise is very elementary.
So, what you're saying is that it's easy to do and therefore it's appropriate? And that it gets the point across and makes the person doing the experiment have the necessary experience in order to become aware of how the mind works? That's great!

62034890
>>62034490
Reread your posts
>>62034856
Maybe for a few. Better than none I guess

62034963
>>62034890
Right... So your point is?

62035196
>>62034381
grim

62035839
>>62034963
I didn't have one

62036344
>>62035839
Ok. So, ... You wanna talk about anything? Anything on your mind?

You said
>Maybe for a few. Better than none I guess
What would you like to know about the mind, if anything? If you don't think the exercise is appropriate then it's because you already understand, right? And if you tell me what you don't understand then we can start moving in that direction, right? Or don't tell me. It's up to you

62037187
>>62034101
>178 replies
>105 pbtid

oh I dunno

62037296
>>62037187
Gavin is paying me to keep the thread alive. This is how we live in Mumbai my good sir. We shill coins while talking about the mind.

62037323
>>62037187
I need rupees to worship the Vishnu on the blockchain my good sar. And to feed my village of Mumbai, the city of DOT.

62037509
>>62037296
>>62037323

Post hand

62039073
>>62037509
I already did here >>62008295. True Mumbai warrior hand. My fathers name was Kumar, my mothers name was Lakshmi. Pic related. I'm the little prince

62039085
>>62037509
I am now a Vishnu warrior of the blockchain, living in the city of DOT, Mumbai. I run a scam call center, they call me papa Rupee over here. I make many Rupees for many villages. We feed whole of city of DOT, Mumbai. We eat very good. Our bellies are very full.

62040207
>>62039073
>>62039085

Shitposting or on hard drugs constantly?

62040932
>>62040207
I don't get the question? Help me understand what you mean. And thanks for bumping the thread. Gavin Wood, our lord and saviour, will be very pleased with you.

May the blockchain Vishnu be with you good sar

62041893
>>62040932
Read again slowly

62042472
>>62041893
Yea..... You're asking me if I'm shitposting or on hard drugs constantly? That's your question? I can't decipher your half-formed sentences to be honest.

62043369
>>62042472
Simple yes or no

62044274
>>62043369
I can't answer if you can't formulate and define what the question is, right? So, please define the question and then I can answer it

62044411
i'm very sad for this investment

62044561
>>62044411
That sounds really tough my fren. Siddartha Gautama said "Attachment is the root cause of all suffering.". Just practice detachment and sooner or later you'll be able to accept your loss. Maybe even grief it if it's a big loss

item.title: Why are crypto millionaires not more common? — 15 replies — 54.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why are crypto millionaires not more common?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-28 13:13 UTC

OP

Serious question. Will just talk about bitcoin here cause that was the easiest.

>become aware of bitcoin in 2013 at peak of the bull, not everyone but most Redditors or 4channers would have been aware
>do a bit of reading, see there weren't any truly convincing arguments against it, like there was stuff of concern but nothing which strongly suggested it was going to go to 0 and not recover
>see the historical performance and realise if it came anywhere close you could easily make massive returns
>go for it and wait, or if really high IQ notice the cyclical pattern early and make an ungodly amount timing that

I timed it terribly initially, didn't buy/sell the cycle (until most recent one where sold at $110k) and became a millionaire 1 year ago at 26. The only thing I did really well was invest a tonne into it for a 19yo (£25k self made). If you are 30 or older it was straight up trivial and you didn't even need to do this.

Yet when you look around it is so rare for anyone to have made serious money from it despite the massive awareness and accessibility. Seriously when I sold and rang the bank for high value transfer the lady said she has never saw anyone with as much as me at only £1 mil.

Early replies (first 3)

62038969
>>62038964
survivorship bias informs your thinking

62038974
>>62038964
We ve got rugged by a faggott.

62038975
I think most rich people keep to themselves and aren't loud so that's probably why you think there aren't many of them.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62038980
>>62038964
Im currently 18 and made 10 million from fartcoin (self made). You dont see me bragging but im here to shit on the parade heh

62038985
>>62038964
>be little 15 years shithead
>have too much time on my hands
>someone on 4chan posts about bitcoin and how it will "revolutionize everything"
>bitcoin is 105$ at the time
>start reading into it
>start reading into current monetary system
>start reading into "Satoshi Nakamoto" aka the CIA
>start reading into the whitepaper and blockchain
>realize in few years time the elite will shift over to crypto and this is just a next step in their way of control
>tell my dad to make a loan in the bank and put it in bitcoin
>"dad, I will be your slave and work tirelessly to give you back 300% even if the investment flops"
>show charts
>give hour long presentations
>he just listens and says "nah"
>am 30 now
>bitcoin is 70k now
>my father regularly says "I should have listened to you"

listen to me now you shitheads
by MVRV z score and rainbow log chart there is no better time than today
couple that with fall of petrodollar, rising inflation and shifting geopolitical power, introduction of CBDCs and myriad of other problems like strait of Hormuz fuck me if btc will not be 300k by the end of the year (minimum)
In 2 years time? 1.5 mill a bitcoin

62038986
>>62038964
>25k
>19
>"self made"
just scanned your post but I already know you're the fag who spams 50 copypasta paragraph "I just inherited 1.2 million, why is everyone else so poor" type faggot threads a day. Stop clogging up the catalog, cuck.

62039007
>>62038985
But why didn't you buy afterwards? It was going for $1k or less until you were like 20, you could easily have just got some McJob and chucked it in instead of relying on parents.

>>62038986
All me bud, it's not easy but didn't require massive brainpower all you had to do was grind and save hard. My mother was on bennies/unemployed most my childhood. In USA probably much easier to accomplish too.

62039016
>>62038985
>fuck me if btc will not be 300k by the end of the year
looks like fucking is in your future

62039023
>>62039007
I was a poor fuck until I hit 28
I live in a second world country
literally all my money went to survival until last 2 years

>>62039016
screencap it and when year ends i will drop my address so you can personally come and buttfuck me, you faggot

62042008
>>62038964
Basically boomers/older ppl don't like higher risk investments (yes, crypto) and would rather buy bonds or something faggy like that. Young people were either too poor, young, or stupid to get in in 2013.

62042048
>>62038964
>go for it and wait
This right here is/was the issue.. It requires some serious conviction or delusion to hodl through all these years.
To get to a million, you have to hodl through ½ million, which most people that didn't drank the coolaid wouldn't be able to.
No casual crypto investor would have made it, it was all laser eyes or people that otherwise believed in it.

62044538
>>62038964
>If you are 30 or older it was straight up trivial
thanks you, captain hindsight
>be early 2013, watch max keiser talk about bitcoin.
>shut up and take my money!
>try to buy with credit card or paypal
>hurr durr you cant, but you can buy thru secondlife paying a massive premium.
>yeah naw.wmf
>try bitstamp they ask for id
>send id
>now proof of residence
>send bank statement.pdf
>no, it needs to be a scan of a real document
>fuck this .bin
later that year i ended up forging an energy bill and they accepted that, right in time for me to buy the top.
welp, my consolation is that i held and didnt sell at the 200 usd bottom.

62044547
>>62038964
Because getting rich from the comfort of your room excludes all the typical humiliation rituals you have to go through to make money in real life

It's too good to be true

62044552
>>62038964
I had about 2k BTC in 2010 from mining on my home computer. I was a teenager so I got fed up with not being able to play my video games and stopped. I eventually reformatted my HDD to make room for games.
Long story short I watched every bull run and seethed instead of just buying in. Hell I even remember seeing litecoin for pennies and ignored it thinking it'll go nowhere. Same with ETH. I've just watched and the massive crashes only reinforced that.
Good thing for me to think about is, if I kept my BTC I would have at best sold it at like 4 dollars because 8k is a lot to a late teenager. That's why most (all?) people who once owned BTC are not millionaires. You would have to be literally brain damaged to hold that shit for 15 years.

62044565
>>62038964
There are way more millionaires from crypto on this board than you realize. And a lot of millionaires have also left this board, as they don't need to put money into risk-on assets anymore

62044575

62044578

62044583

item.title: I scheduled a meeting with my financial planner to discuss potentially reallocating my portfolio in response to the Iran… — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I scheduled a meeting with my financial planner to discuss potentially reallocating my portfolio in response to the Iran…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-28 13:27 UTC

OP

I scheduled a meeting with my financial planner to discuss potentially reallocating my portfolio in response to the Iran war.

I am tempted to reveal my power level during our call. It will take discipline for me not to use the term “ZOG” when we talk.

Should I be candid and see what he says? Or should I stay discreet?

My planner is not Jewish FYI (but his boss probably is).

Please advise.

Early replies (first 3)

62043737
>>62043698
You don't need to justify or explain shit to someone you're hiring. Only bad things can happen if you do. He's not going to think "this guy i based, I should give him my first rate advice"

62043835
>>62043698
ZOG will be a term used by normies in 5 months. Screen cap this.

62043848
just do it. aren't you bored? I am. No more filter. It's fun. I'm shorting life.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62043850
Candid how? De-risking in response to the war is reasonable, wouldn't have thought much more detail than that one sentence is needed

62043851
>>62043698
Why? what do you expect to gain from it?
Person will have no idea what you mean and keep going with his recommended plan or 2, he will know and be professional and side step your comment barely acknowledging it.
>t. business major

62044034
>>62043698
Retard.

62044526
>>62043850
>De-risking in response to the war is reasonable
It was reasonable back in January. It's too late now.

62044539
>>62043835
Kanye West - "It's not that I'm not right, sometimes I'm just not right now"
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ib_lVNaoPPU&list=RDib_lVNaoPPU&start_radio=1

62044550
Why bother? Are you retiring within the next 5-10 years? If not ignore everything and continue to DCA as normal, at best buy more than you normally would after heavy dips.

62044598
>>62043698

> power level

What is wrong with you?

62044257

62044288

>>62044108
The Saudis aint shit. They hire subsaharan mercenaries and need westerners to maintain their airforce.

NOOOO YEMEN PLEASE DONT FIRE ROCKETS MADE OUT OF EMPTY BEER CANS AND CHILI POWDER AT ISRAEL, THINK ABOUT THE WORLD ECONOMY!

item.title: Where do I put my money? — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Where do I put my money?

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-28 13:22 UTC

OP

As some of you already know, crypto is a scam, real-estate is in bubble, stocks are massively above their fair values. I have no interrest in any of these.

Where do I put my money to risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years???

Im all in Pokemon vintage cards right now.

BTW If you read this you will make it.

Early replies (first 3)

62044209
>Where do I put my money to risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years???
The casino.

If you are looking for actually realistic returns then stocks is probably still the best bet, despite being overvalued. Especially now that they have fallen a bit recently. Personally I would choose value stocks rather than growth or tech stocks though. And you should also keep some in cash/bonds to be prepared to buy in more if the stock market falls further.

62044233
>>62044195
>risk it all for 5-20x in 5 years
all-in on monero and bitcoin cash

62044282
>>62044195
Personally im all in funko pops and labubus. Not financial advice but dont say i didnt warn ya

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62044318
>>62044195
Antique Persian rugs

62044588
>>62044233
>monero
> bitcoin cash

trash.

item.title: 200W MA BITCOIN — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
200W MA BITCOIN

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-28 13:25 UTC

OP

Oldfag here. Remember. Don't start accumulating until BTC goes under the 200w ma like every single cycle before.
Right now it's at 58k.
it could go lower but thats always a good starting point for the next run.

62043880
The numbers don't lie, only thing that scares me is this war going on.

62044135
left is me and right is my asian gf.

62044511

>miss out on accumulating because the price is barely 8% higher than what it "should" be
>when the price will thousands of times higher in coming decades

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62044534
>>62044135
mogasex

62044592
>>62044534
Kek there's only like 100 people left on /biz/ but two are goobas huh.

Attached is the non-retard analysis.See you at 30k.

62044507

62044536

item.title: >stocks down >Gold down >Silver down >Index funds down >BTC down What the hell are we supposed to do? — 39 replies — 45.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>stocks down >Gold down >Silver down >Index funds down >BTC down What the hell are we supposed to do?

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-28 12:37 UTC

OP

>stocks down
>Gold down
>Silver down
>Index funds down
>BTC down
What the hell are we supposed to do? Accumulate cash and eat 14% inflation?

Early replies (first 3)

62040441
>>62040435
>he didn’t buy land

62040443
Stock food and fuel

62040448
>>62040435
>What the hell are we supposed to do >Accumulate cash and eat 14% inflation?
Yes. Then you time the bottom. Probably around September.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62040566
>>62040559
The total for energy was 0.5% you nigger. And who cares about medical care costs, if you're not a retard you'll have insurance that covers it.

62040992
>>62040435
I voted for this

62040996
>>62040435
yes? stacking cash is not necessarily a bad thing
warren buffet is all cash right now dude

62041004
>>62040526
>believing the 2.4% number

>>62040566
calling others retarded

62041043
>>62040526

CPI will be 5% next report and 7% the report after

62041049
>>62041043
k mr. buffet. if you are so certain about inflation hitting record numbers why dont you just short inflation? how about that?

62041260
>>62040566
>Calls others retard
>Insurance just means you don't pay medical bills

62041314
>>62040435
travel the world, buy your favourite car, live your life
meme numbers on the internet don't matter

62041317
>>62041260
That's literally what insurance does you retard. You pay premiums so that when you need it the insurance company covers your bills.

62041445
>>62041004
>I'm le uber smart conspiracy theorist

62041554
Convert to Judaism

Seriously though, oil stocks were the cheapest sector of the S&P at the start of the year, so even a retard like myself was in them before the war.

62041563
>>62040435

XOM

62042054
>>62041317
>t. a zoomer who has never had his own health insurance

62042070
>>62040435
>Oh no I would sure hate to buy at these cheap prices!
>I only buy at premium prices!

Buy you fucking idiot

62042442
>>62040526
Hahahha hahah hahahah

62042475
>>62040435
im starting a reverse mortgage business where i give 1% off to boomers if they let me kill their lawn before they move out

62042646
>>62042070
>please just please buy my baggies, my diamond hands can't hold any longer
Now is the worst time to enter, You are too late on oil, unless you want highish risk and mediocre to high returns. You are too early to buy anything else, since depending on how this retarded Iran debacle is going to turn out there is a significant risk that the markets will crash. It all depends on the retards at the white house.

But this is /biz/ so buy high and sell low.

62042654
>>62042646
>Buying when the price goes down is the worst time to buy!
lmao the absolute state of biztards

62042662
>>62042054
I don't know where you live but in france but whole fucking point of health insurance is to cover most of your expensive medical bills, if not all.

62042665
>>62042654
The price is going to continue to go down, if you think this is anywhere close the bottom then you trully deserve those baggies.

62042669
Gold litterally rock eting so buy it

62042742
>>62042662
france lmao.
in usa, you have a family deductible of $24,000 even after paying for "insurance"

62042793
>>62042665
>The price is going down
>IT'S JUST GOING TO KEEP GOING DOWN AAA IT'S ALL OVER SELL SELL SELL!!1
>The price is going up
>IT'S JUST GOING TO KEEP GOING UP OOOOO BUY BUY BUY WAGMI

Lmao. Never change biztards. Never change.

62042803
>>62040566
The last CPI report was for February you absolute retard

62043941
>>62040435
next the hyperinflation. your cash will be worth nothing.

62044428
>>62040435
I prefer the original

62044434
>>62040441
it's dumping harder than everything OP listed. Don't look at your zillow lil' bro.

62044438
>>62040435
long oil

62044446
>>62040435
live stock of food and cotton discs

62044490
>>62040435
Oil. Wheat and Beef. In that order over time.

item.title: How do people do it, I don't understand. — 32 replies — 18.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do people do it, I don't understand.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-03-28 11:43 UTC

OP

How do people do it, I don't understand. This can't be it...

I graduated college in December and finally got an office job for the first time. Nothing great, but hey at least it's something. The first two weeks I was loving it, I finally got my own place, a paycheck, eager to learn and prove myself. I felt happy I was finally able to get out of my parents place and move out into the real world.

It didn't take long for that to wear off though, noticed when I got home I had no energy to work out and just wanted to watch tv or play a casual video game. Then I realized, this will be the rest of my life. Just going to work, coming home, and being a wagecuck in an apartment. All of my co-workers are in their 50's and later so theres no one to actually befriend. Now that my orientation is done I'm lucky to have 2-3 hours of actual work to do and then look busy the rest of the time. I did some calculations and at most I'll be able to save like 8 grand, a year.

Sure, yeah, I have a job, insurance, and shelter but what the fuck. How on earth do people do this for 30+ years? I can already feel my soul eroding. My whole life I was told to get in the real world and to get out there, and it SUCKS. What a lie. Boomers at least got a house, car, wife, kids, community, cheap college, and burned it all to enrich themselves some more.

Early replies (first 3)

62038528
tl;dr

62038530
>>62038518
Yea youre supposed to have kids in this normie cookie cutter pipeline, sorry sweaty. Oh and a divorce comes with the package aswell.

62038534
>>62038518
Not true at all

The older you get the less energy you have and I'm not talking about your 80s. You're gonna lose energy fast starting around 40. Also your health goes to shit.

You are in the BEST years of adulthood. It gets much worse from here

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62038541
>>62038518
want to start a business with me? I have the time and no money

62038557
The best part is everyone pretends to love it lmfao. Yes mr. mcdonald, I love my job!

62038626
>>62038518
Well. Do something you'd do for free as a job. That really changes things.
You might even paid well because you have dedicated so much time into the craft that you become one of the best at it.

And make it something challenging you can grow better at, keeps it fun.

I did exactly that and it's been very nice.

62038627
>>62038518
Well, atleast you have the weekend bro.
Just get fucked up with friends on weekends to cope.

62038633
>>62038534
This is only really accurate for people who let themselves go. I feel much better physically than I did 10-15 years ago ad a teenager. I didn’t do any cardio back then and my diet was much worse by comparison. I haven’t lost the ability to really do anything since then, life only gets better if you want it to.

62038634
>>62038518

62038636
Can you shut the fuck up and stop posting this copypasta bullshit on /biz/ all the fucking time.

62038638
>>62038633
Keep telling yourself that. It's important to keep your hopes up

62038674
>>62038518
I am going to let you in on a little secret people seem to be completely ignorant of
>time moves faster the older you get
It may be hard to notice now, but once you start looping day after day in a closed loop and dont improve yourself, days will feel like weeks and weeks months, months to years. Next thing you know 20 years have passed and you made no progress in your dead end job. This is the trait of a consumer. Do you really need all the things others tell you you need? Reality is an IQ test, anon

62038679
>>62038534
go to the gym

62038871
>>62038518
they dont. i was like you, graduated in 2009 grinded for like 15 years then became NEET during covid moved back in with my parents and am still NEET to this day. never going back sorry i mean i know i should because boomers israel and migrants need my taxes but im just not gonna hahahahaha

62038900
Be a neet. If you're parents are decent boomers, they must have enough money for you to live a somewhat decent life without working.

That's what I'm doing. I'm sorry but I worked for a few months and I'm not gonna live like that. Let the tamed goyim deal with the real world.

62039456
>>62038518
>8732472th instance of zoom zoom learns the "value" of working

>>62038636
I don't remember seeing it before, but thanks for letting us know

62040230
>>62038518
Youre paid because its shit, not because its nice, dumbass. People arent being paid for visiting amusement parks, they pay for it.

62040461
The name of the game is ... Real estate. Start buying a home, then a carwash, then a rental property and so on.

62040479
>>62038518
>no energy
Biology varies but I was a non-sporty twig when I started doing squats - the logic is legs hold up everything else. Over time went from 50 in like 30 min to over 115 in 15 minutes regularly. Being in better shape had me feeling more energetic in general.
>30+ years
Investment research. Found the various 10x, a 30x and multiple 100x because I spent time doing the boring, mind-melting research. /biz/ frequently told me to do my own research to when I asked questions. Just like you I missed plenty of good opportunities but the only way to find is to be looking at all. You only have to be right so many times as your gains multiply.

62040491
>>62038633
>10-15 years ago ad a teenager
So you're not old enough yet to start falling apart. It starts in late 30s
>heh I'll just lift
Not sure how that'll help with absolutely random problems you'll start getting. Like I hurt a nerve in my arm recently and for about a month my fingers were numb. The kind of shit that doesn't happen when you're 25

62040516
>>62038528
TL;DR:
You got your first office job and independence, were excited at first, but quickly felt drained, bored, and unfulfilled. Now you’re worried this repetitive work–home routine is all life will be, with little social connection or financial progress, and it’s making you question how people tolerate it long-term.

62041685
>>62040461
a home is like 600k where i am, that will take a lifetime

62041980
>>62038633
Yeah you started off from a baseline of shit so it looks like you're getting better, if you were actually in shape and active when you were young you would have a real point of comparison and would be able to see just how bad your 'better physically' is compared to where you were before.

>>62038674
I think you meant to say: weeks will feel like days, months will feel like weeks and years will feel like months. Covid didn't help at all, everyone has basically checked out since then and are just going through the motions.

62041995
>>62040491
>>62038534
kek why do zoomzooms think you die once you turn 40

62042103
>>62041995
earlier if you're a safe and effective member

62044226
>>62038518
>Person living in USA, Canada, Western Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Japan or South Korea writes blog post about how they're suffering
Kek

62044232
>>62038900
>>62038871
>The solution to the Jew Hellscape that their Golem enforcers (White "people") perpetuate is to parasite your own parents who themselves make a living by being good goys

62044244
>>62038518
see the trick here is that society is built around households and dual income and you are basing your earnings on single income, if you had a partner or wife who also worked then almost all of her income would be able to be dedicated to investments/better housing/kids etc

There's a reason the government bases its economic surveys on household income and not individual income, two people working and pooling their money together have a lot more spending power.

62044273
>>62044244
Mine is a single earner household, making a fair amount. Women don't need or even want to be in the workplace. It actually ruins everything for everyone except a very small percentage of people at the top who make more money and can flee to other countries when things get really bad. Reject this system of oppression, women should not be in the workforce. My wife is at home gardening or doing art, and although it would be nice to have some extra money, what is money for if your wife can't even play in the dirt or draw with paper and a few markers. This is the reality of end-stage exploitation of the masses.

62044321
>>62038534
link marines are getting old, it's sad to see

62044351
>>62038534
wtf i'm 25 and i'm already at 0.1% battery left. I can barely crawl out of bed most days, how am i supposed to handle the rest of my life if i'm more energy depleted than most 80yo boomers?

62044369
>>62038627
>friends
Where do you think you are?

item.title: What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday. — 17 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-28 12:24 UTC

OP

What the fuck is going on with MSFT?
How is this not a great entry?
Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Early replies (first 3)

62043952
because the music has stopped

fucking cretin

62043962
Just buy gold and gold mining stocks.

62043971
>>62043947
I would wait until i see signs of of absolute panic.
And even then only go in for a medium term trade.
Liquidity is not great and there are big IPOs coming that will absorb even more of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62043976
Because everyone is finally and unironically ditching windows
Windows doesn't make them money, but it's a window to everything that does
Enterprises ditching MS will start to get priced in, don't think it has happened yet

62043981
>>62043947
>>62043952
>>62043962
>>62043971
>>62043976
Because Sailer is a retard who always buys the top

It is gonna going much more down

BITCOIN is over rally

62043992
microjeetslop is an indian company now, im shorting it down to like 250

62044011
>>62043947
Woah, we put Jeets in charge and nothing works!?!?!?!? How is that possible chat? How is that possible!?!?!?! H1B is our greatest ally.

62044040
>>62043947
@ 201

62044042
>>62043947
It's a great entry. I've been shorting Microslop on /smg/ the whole time. Have you been paying attention?

62044047
>>62043981
>sailer bought msft
how many streets did you shit on today ?

62044154
>>62043947
>How is this not a great entry?
we don't invest in India for a good reason, because its risky

62044170
>>62043962

And aluminium (CENTURY ALUMINIUM) and copper (ARIZONA SONORAN COPPER COMPANY INC - FARADAY COPPER CORP) and rare earth (FIREWEED - LYNAS - ACLARA - CAMECO) helium (DESERT MOUNTAIN - HELIX EXPLORATION - PULSAR - IACX)

All the critical metals and essential industrial commodities are massively underpriced for the next few years.

62044171
miggersooft is either going to bottom out at that double bottom or break the fuck down back to 2022 lows hope you're good on that lol.

62044208
200 is fair price

62044255
>>62044170
Why are they underpriced? Doesn't industrial production crater during a bear?

62044335
>>62043947
$ 150 is the entrypoint. MSFT is fucked right now. They exported to many jobs to India. Indians wont't come to work because of power outages, hunting for food and energy. India shut down fertilizer plant due lack of resources, it is going a lot worse.

Also MSFT once again failed to implement a new technology, Zune failed, Nokia phones failed and now they just wasted to much money on copilot which is just bad software.

62044463
>>62044208
Possible but will probably bounce earlier, around 220-250.

>>62044335
It's not reaching $150 before a global market crash, you doom schizo. In such a scenario you could just buy better stocks for cheap.

item.title: Who’s buying BTC at the dip? — 49 replies — 14.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who’s buying BTC at the dip?

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-03-28 10:03 UTC

OP

Dip incoming, time to stack or wait it out?
Share your moves, bags, or strategies. Let’s see who’s taking advantage of the dip.

Early replies (first 3)

62025264
>>62025259
Might wait another couple of months...

62025266
>>62025264
Try a year.

62025274
>>62025264
Like how many months?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62031831
chomo coin aint going back up blud

62033377
>>62030098
That's funny

62033836
>>62025259
I'm buying.
Time in the market better than timing the market I always say.

62033874
>>62031290
Let’s see how low BTC goes this year. Still stacking and staking mine on Babylon. Better than just letting it sit.

62034106
>>62033836
Buy more
>>62033874
hold is the best option

62034113
>>62034106
I think staking somewhere is just another shit

62034130
>>62031831
Whats chomo coin

62034516
>>62034106
Holding is fine, but why not earn on it now that native staking exists? Think about it, anon.

62034519
>>62031274
Well, depends on you anon

62034531
>>62034113
Sounds like you mean centralized platforms. This isn’t just some random place to stake, it’s designed differently, maintaining our control.

62035042
>>62025418
You can never catch the bottom, just DCA and stake on a good BTCFI that keeps BTC Native

62035048
>>62034519
It only makes sense to hold right now if you don't have time to play the game. But it doesn't take much effort to set buy orders at 69k, sell orders at 70/71k, and watch the money roll in. It's easy money right now.

62036008
>BTC
I bought DOGE at the dip. It's gonna moon this time for sure.

62036354
>>62031293
You're doing the right move ser

62038277
>>62035042
That’s what I’m doing as well, anon. I wonder why they haven’t realized this approach yet. But it’s not too late anyway.

62038755
>>62035048
You also have an option to earn from that instead of just holding with no return

62038963
>>62036008
doge

Lol

62038989
>>62033874
bitcoin defi is interesting

62039027
>>62038989
wrapped btc isn't defi

62039045
>>62025259
Hold till $1M

62040086
>>62038989
Yeah, it is. You should also look into Trustless Bitcoin Vaults, which will open up more ways to use BTC without giving up control.
>>62039027
Yup, I know, but that’s not what I meant. You can check it again.

62040127
>>62039045
I'd love to know that

62040628
>>62025259
>What about bch

62040638

ICP
ETH
all dead

62040666
>>62040628

62040670
gimme more cash orange boy

62040730
>>62025259

>>62025264

62043271
>>62025264

62044082
>>62040628
I'm not buying

62044190
>>62025292

item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-03-28 10:52 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62040721

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62040977
>>62040911
>micro-tradegy
fucking kek

62041405
can he just fast forward and hang himself now instead of waiting 2 years

62041421

62041459
>>62041421
2 retards dont make a right

62041479
>>62038894
how do i give this guy my money

62041552
>>62041479
You convinced me, I wanna know too

62041934
Some other anon in /smg/ told me they're aiming for a 11% dividend yield or something, but all i see is a CEO that post cringe tweets. Must be tough bagholding this

62042057
>>62041421
Lmao

62042072
>>62041934
Extremely bullish post

62042260
>>62041934
Where the fuck would the money even come from?

62042308
>>62038894
He is still up billions with his Micro Strategy Stock

62042319
>>62042260
bitcoin is going to go up because of the rainbow chart and because its GOING TO GO UP BECAUSE THERE'S ONLY HECKIN 21M BITCUNTS OUT THERE

62043624
>>62040752
Saylor, what are you doing here?

62043627
>>62040752
well said. it sucks dealing with all these sub 130 IQ cattle. imagine unironically shilling for the dollar and jewish banking system lmao

62043648
Whenever I see somebody wearing their own merch I immediately short their stock

62043658
>>62043648
Thank you for your contribution. I look forward to liquidating you

62043660
>>62041934
I told you that. And they aren’t “aiming for it”, they’re doing it. Pop in the /quant/ thread and I’ll sperg out for you.

Do you realize that MSTR was the #1 most profitable company in the US for awhile? They were making something like $500m per day selling Bitcoin volatility through their products.

If you actually look into the capital/debt structure you’ll see they’re in a stellar position. If you want to be long BTC, MSTR is just the better choice. It’s an engine that accretes BTC, and they’re going to pivot to banking at some point.

62043666
>>62042260
They already have it. If you don’t know how MSTR makes money, maybe you should research it?

62043720
>>62038894
Kek

62043746
>>62038894
The problem is that this retard doesn't DCA. He keeps buying local tops in order to keep the price up. He barely averages down. Why didn't he buy at 60k when he recently had a chance? He waited like a retard for it to recover.
>>62041934
>>62043660
NEOS' BTCI gets you more than double that. They also have NEHI (Ethereum) which yields 35% kek.

62043754
>>62038894
tom lee thinks those are rookie numbers

62043767
>>62043746
>an illiquid leveraged ETF is the same as a liquid BTC accretion engine
itsallsotiresome.jpg

Also, they buy tops because that's when volatility is highest and they have the most premium/leverage to strip. Please take your IQ takes to /r/buttcoin

62043788
>>62043767
/r/iamverysmart
And what happens to MSTR if Bitcoin goes sup 30k for 3 years?

62043861
his ponzi thing was only above par and buying bitcoin for a couple days this entire month which means he has to pay everyone out of his cash reserves

62043863
>>62043788
>And what happens to MSTR if Bitcoin goes sup 30k for 3 years?
Literally nothing, they could half that price for twice as long at current reserves and obligations. Not hard to figure this shit out yourself.

62043993
>>62038894
Michael Saylor is retarded. Even Epstein called him a retard. It seems obvious to anyone who isn't a retard that this guy isn't worth the attention.

62044254
>>62043627
Bitcoin is a jewish ponzi scheme and they want you to buy it instead of land, gold or guns

62044260
>>62038894
Just short when Gaylord buys.
Go all in when he gets liquidated.

Simple as

item.title: What's going to replace the US dollar? I don't think we'll get the PetroYuan at least any time soon even in this generat… — 33 replies — 13.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
What's going to replace the US dollar? I don't think we'll get the PetroYuan at least any time soon even in this generat…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-03-28 10:59 UTC

OP

What's going to replace the US dollar? I don't think we'll get the PetroYuan at least any time soon even in this generation, it's more likely we'll be going back to Gold and Silver standards as the entire economy sharts itself.

Early replies (first 3)

62039586
>>62039580
Didn't they say in Agenda 2030 or the Great Reset or something that we're switching to Central Bank Digital Currency?

62039592
>>62039580
dogecoin clearly

62039597
>>62039580
CBDCs
This is obviously controlled demolition, US empire is on exit door for long time now. CBDCs, IDs to verify identity using any and all OS, constant CCTV monitoring.... we are going to a cyberpunk dystopia but without cool augments
That's why Elon and Trump had love hate relationship, because it was about this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62039605
>>62039580
Rubels

62039677
>>62039580
The USD chart looks bullish to me actually, honestly the whole Iran situation is big bullish for the dollar imo, it's completel.y fucking voer the energy markets in Americas largest competitors, Asia and Europe, meanwhile the US has just started receiving large amounts of oil from Venezuela and is already energy independent.

62039886
>>62039580
Ether

62039911
I don't know how anyone can still have faith in this shit
https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0425

62039988
>>62039580
There's not enough gold and silver in the world to replace the dollar. Physical metals just don't work at the scale and speed that the world needs.
I do feel like there has to be something, some invention, that could solve the medium of exchange issue for world trade so that countries don't have to rely on a single nation's economy and its currency to be able to trade with each other. Some economist will invent it at some point in the future and everybody will be like how didn't we think of this earlier.

62040044
>>62039988
>There's not enough gold and silver in the world to replace the dollar

This never made sense to me, if there's not enough then you just make it really expensive and divide it into smaller amounts like with BTC right?

62040053
>>62039677
>muh lines on a chart
you're no better than astrologist

62040079
>>62040044
I think you want to be able to produce new currency when it's needed, otherwise, there won't be enough money in the system to go around and economic growth slows. If this sounds hand-wavy, it's because I don't understand it properly myself. Also, you can't instantly wire 5 tons of gold between banks like you can with currency. You have to fly it to the destination bank, which takes time, money, and exposes the gold to theft, which means you have to hire heavy security, which increases cost.

62040283
>>62039597
> IDs to verify identity using any and all OS, constant CCTV monitoring
... Palantir...

62040299
>>62039988

XRP

62040300
>>62039580
No single reserve currency but rather you can trade in multiple currencies like the USD, Euro, Yen, etc

62040467
>>62039592
>>62039886
>>62040299

no.

The correct answer is Bitcoin. Everything else is wrong.

62040476
>>62040299
>>62040467
Nobody cares about your shitty little ponzi coins.

62040499
>>62039586
This literally wouldn't work because of shit like conflict minerals and general 3rd world. How are the Africans mining coltan in the middle of an ethnic genocide for Semiconductor Inc. going to get paid in Dem.Congo with digital stuff.

62040502
>>62040499
Tatted white women?

62040556
>>62040476

Uniformed commentary is for people who enjoy poverty.

62040562
>>62039580
Goypoints.

62040600
>>62040556
Bro, even if the central banks wanted to use crypto, they'd just pick one and clone it. They're not going to pay you anything for your imaginary internet funny money.

62040622
>>62040600

> central banks

How's the weather in Tel Aviv, rabbi?

62040632
>>62039580
>What's going to replace the US dollar?

Nothing, everyone else is in a worse off state than the US with massive demographic bubbles and they're inflating their way out.

62040653
>>62039580
>it's more likely we'll be going back to Gold and Silver standards
this is baggie cope
pms are just another asset to be pumped and dumped, nothing more

62040674
>>62039580
Government backed stablecoins
Have you not been following the news over the past couple years? Every major economic market is trying to kill money and circumvent banking to achieve total control.

62040684
>>62039677
The usd value is the result of a trust engine. The moment the qorld stops trusting it as a reserve and/or trade currency, it will become argentinian peso-tier. They are going to have a worse currency devaluation spiral than the late roman empire.

62040693
>>62039592
The only correct answer.

62041375
>>62039988
They already did invent it and it's been running continuously for over 17 years.

62041515
>>62039597
All political parties, feds and billionaires who collude with feds along w big tech want to do this and we’re all moving towards this so they can disenfranchise people with stasi shit without even firing a shot.

62042255
>>62039597
>US empire
Here's that meme again. There is no such thing. You fell for the neocon rhetoric + Hollywood theatrics but can't let go.

62042497
>>62039580
Gold and silver are not feasible as a form of currency in a world of 9-10b people.
It's almost certainly going to be some form of digital currency, likely in the form of CBDC's.

62042643
>>62039580
>What's going to replace the US dollar?
Last time I checked, the US dollar was just a specific weight of silver or gold, I think your reference is to the Federal Reserve's notes

62044279
>>62040674
why would the government create stablecoins, that would be retarded as fuck, if anything the government would actively ban stablecoins (like china) or just tolerate their existence (like USA)
